# middleware flag with --output-multiple-files: per-file route functions
# receive the middleware and wrap their handlers

muxt generate --output-multiple-files --output-routes-func-with-middleware-param

exists index_template_routes_gen.go
exists about_template_routes_gen.go

exec go test -count=1

-- go.mod --
module example.com

go 1.24
-- template.go --
package server

import (
	"embed"
	"html/template"
)

//go:embed *.gohtml
var templateFiles embed.FS

var templates = template.Must(template.ParseFS(templateFiles, "*.gohtml"))
-- index.gohtml --
{{define "GET /{$}"}}<h1>Hello</h1>{{end}}
-- about.gohtml --
{{define "GET /about"}}<h1>About</h1>{{end}}
-- template_test.go --
package server

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"
)

func TestMiddlewareAppliesToAllTemplateFiles(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	var patterns []string
	TemplateRoutes(mux, nil, func(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
		return http.HandlerFunc(func(res http.ResponseWriter, req *http.Request) {
			patterns = append(patterns, req.Pattern)
			next.ServeHTTP(res, req)
		})
	})

	for _, path := range []string{"/", "/about"} {
		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, path, nil)
		rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
		mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)
		if got := rec.Result().StatusCode; got != http.StatusOK {
			t.Errorf("GET %s: status = %d, want %d", path, got, http.StatusOK)
		}
	}
	if len(patterns) != 2 {
		t.Errorf("middleware saw %d requests, want 2: %q", len(patterns), patterns)
	}
}

func TestNilMiddlewareMultipleFiles(t *testing.T) {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()

	TemplateRoutes(mux, nil, nil)

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/about", nil)
	rec := httptest.NewRecorder()
	mux.ServeHTTP(rec, req)

	if got := rec.Result().StatusCode; got != http.StatusOK {
		t.Error("status with nil middleware =", got, "want", http.StatusOK)
	}
}
